page: OVHcloud r2-15 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Core Processor of the Haswell architecture running at 2.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 15.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a bundled 50 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the r2-15 sits in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1098.99 and multi-core score of 2095.09 are categorized as weak and poor respectively. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 22.28 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 15.09 GB/sec. In database and key-value operations, the instance achieved 3.07 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a Passmark database score of 1436.35, reflecting its entry-level capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r2-15 offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances, without the premium cost of modern high-performance hardware. However, the tradeoff lies in its reliance on older Intel Haswell architecture and low memory bandwidth, which limits its suitability for intensive data processing. This instance is best suited for lightweight database hosting, Redis caching, basic web serving, and small-scale memory-bound applications that do not require high computational throughput.
description: OVHcloud r2-15 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Haswell processor running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 15.0 GB of RAM and a 50 GB local SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1104.0 and a multi-core score of 1952.0, placing its compute and memory bandwidth capabilities in the lower percentiles. While the older CPU architecture limits its raw processing power, the instance offers a cost-effective option for memory-allocated workloads that do not demand high CPU throughput. The r2-15 is best suited for lightweight database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and basic memory-bound applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly, memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated vCPUs and a high RAM-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
